name: Current Affairs in Indian Economy & TN Dev. Admin. (4): India's 2026 Free Trade Agreements

sourceRefs.label: aljazeera.com/weforum.org - India-EU Free Trade Agreement (concluded 27 Jan 2026); India-New Zealand FTA (27 Apr 2026); US-India tariff deal (2 Feb 2026)

researchNotes: Fourth Economy current-affairs topic (4th lap). Picked 3 dated, concluded/signed trade agreements over RBI repo-rate data (which, while stable at 5.25% through Feb-June 2026, is thinner/less distinctive exam content than a landmark, decades-in-the-making trade deal). Expansion pass (Batch 16): added the India-UK CETA — India's most comprehensive FTA with a G-7 nation — rounding out the year's trade-agreement picture to 4 major deals (EU, New Zealand, US, UK).

reading.intro: Covers 3 major Free Trade Agreements India concluded in 2026 — its most active year yet for trade diplomacy.

qa.question: What major trade agreement did India conclude with the EU, and when?
qa.answer: The India-EU Free Trade Agreement, announced 27 January 2026 — after nearly two decades of negotiations. Nicknamed the 'mother of all deals' for its scale.

qa.question: How large is the India-EU FTA's combined market, and what share of global GDP does it represent?
qa.answer: It covers about 2 billion people, with a combined market value of nearly $27 trillion — about 25% of global GDP.

qa.question: What tariff relief does the India-EU FTA give Indian exporters, and which Indian sectors benefit most?
qa.answer: The EU will eliminate tariffs on 90% of Indian goods immediately (rising to 93% within 7 years). Sectors benefiting with zero tariffs immediately: seafood, chemicals, plastics, leather, textiles, apparel, metals, and gems/jewellery.

qa.question: Which historically protected Indian sector is opening up under the India-EU FTA, and by how much?
qa.answer: The automobile sector — tariffs cut from up to 110% down to 30-35% initially, phasing down further to 10% over time.

qa.question: What trade agreement did India sign with New Zealand, and when?
qa.answer: India and New Zealand signed a Free Trade Agreement on 27 April 2026, giving Indian exporters full duty-free access to the New Zealand market upon entry into force.

qa.question: What tariff change did the US-India trade deal (announced 2 February 2026) bring?
qa.answer: US tariffs on Indian goods were cut from a punitive 50% down to a consolidated 18%.

qa.question: What is the India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), and when was it signed/when does it take effect?
qa.answer: India's most comprehensive trade agreement with a G-7 nation, and the UK's most significant bilateral FTA since leaving the EU. Signed on 24 July 2025, after 14 rounds of negotiations over 3 years — set to come into effect on 15 July 2026.

qa.question: What market access does the India-UK CETA provide, and what is the bilateral trade target?
qa.answer: The UK will immediately eliminate duties on 99% of Indian tariff lines, covering nearly 100% of trade value — including removing tariffs of up to 70% on processed foods and 21.5% on marine products. Current bilateral trade (~USD 56 billion) is targeted to DOUBLE by 2030.

questions.prompt: The India-EU Free Trade Agreement was announced on:

questions.explanation: 30-35% initially, phasing down to 10% over time.
